title: Joe Chan

[[Willie McLean (rugby league)|Willie McLean]] (uncle)
[[Casey McLean]] (cousin) }} }} '''Joseph Chan''' (born 10 March 2002) is a professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sr}} forward for the [[Melbourne Storm]] in the [[National Rugby League]] (NRL). He previously played for [[Catalans Dragons]] in the [[Super League]] and [[Saint-Esteve XIII Catalan|Saint-Esteve]] in the [[Elite One Championship]]. ==Background== Chan is of [[Han Chinese|Chinese]], [[Māori people|Māori]], [[Samoans| Samoan]] and [[Irish people|Irish]] heritage{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2023/01/11/chan-takes-the-path-less-travelled-to-storm/ |title=Chan takes path less travelled to Storm|publisher=NRL.com|date=11 Jan 2023|accessdate=21 Mar 2026}}{{Cite web |url=http://www.hreoc.gov.au/racial_discrimination/voices/nrlstars/civoniceva_chan.html |title=Human Rights and Equal Opportunity Commission Website |access-date=26 September 2012 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110414010247/http://www.hreoc.gov.au/racial_discrimination/voices/nrlstars/civoniceva_chan.html |archive-date=14 April 2011 |url-status=dead }} and is the son of former [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] international [[Alex Chan (rugby league)|Alex Chan]] and brother of [[Tiaki Chan]].{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/catalans-vs-warrington/report/55548|title=Catalans Dragons 6-16 Warrington Wolves: Clinical Wolves move into Challenge Cup semi-finals|publisher=Sky Sports|date=7 May 2021|accessdate=8 May 2021}} He played his junior rugby league for [[St Clair Comets]], before moving to [[Catalans Dragons]]. Chan is first cousins with [[Penrith Panthers]] centre [[Casey McLean]]. Chan attended St.Dominic’s College, Penrith before moving to the south of France to complete his schooling. ==Career== In 2021 he made his [[Catalans Dragons|Catalans]] debut in the [[Challenge Cup]] against the [[Warrington Wolves]].{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/match/EVP3571753|title=Challenge Cup: Catalans Dragons v Warrington Wolves|publisher=BBC|date=7 May 2021|accessdate=8 May 2021}} On 22 April 2022 he signed a two-year contract to play for the Melbourne Storm for the 2023 and 2024 seasons.{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/melbourne-storm-confirm-signings-of-joe-chan-and-tariq-sims |title=Melbourne Storm confirm signings of Joe Chan and Tariq Sims |website=Love Rugby League |date=22 April 2022 |access-date=1 June 2026}} Chan made his NRL debut in round 27 of the [[2023 Melbourne Storm season|2023 NRL season]] for the [[Melbourne Storm]] against [[Brisbane Broncos|Broncos]] at [[Suncorp Stadium]]. Being presented with jersey (cap number 233), Joe along with his father and former [[Melbourne Storm]] (cap number 71), [[Alex Chan (rugby league)|Alex Chan]] are the first father-son Storm duo.{{Cite web |author=Staff Writer |date=2023-08-31 |title=Joe Chan becomes player 233 |url=https://www.melbournestorm.com.au/news/2023/08/31/joe-chan-becomes-player-233/ |access-date=2024-08-27 |website=Melbourne Storm |language=en}} Chan went onto win the [[2023 Queensland Cup]] with Storm feeder club the [[Brisbane Tigers]] where he came off the interchange bench in their 22–18 grand final victory over Burleigh.{{cite web |last1=Brinums |first1=Jorja |title=Tigers pounce to create history with grand final win over Bears |url=https://www.qrl.com.au/news/2023/09/17/tigers-pounce-to-create-history-with-grand-final-win-over-bears/ |website=qrl.com.au |access-date=18 September 2023 |date=17 September 2023}} He would also be recognised as the Tigers Rookie of the Year at the club's annual awards.{{cite web |title=2023 season awards for Hostplus Cup and Hastings Deering Colts Tigers |url=https://thetigers.com.au/brisbane-tigers/2023-season-awards-for-hostplus-cup-and-hastings-deering-colts-tigers/ |website=thetigers.com.au |access-date=26 September 2023 |date=25 September 2023}} On 27 August 2024, Chan re-signed with the Storm until the end of 2028.{{Cite web |author=Staff Writer |date=2024-08-26 |title=Storm's family connection continues |url=https://www.melbournestorm.com.au/news/2024/08/27/storms-family-connection-continues/ |access-date=2024-08-27 |website=Melbourne Storm |language=en}} On 29 September 2024, Chan played for Melbourne's feeder team [[North Sydney Bears|North Sydney]] in their NSW Cup Grand Final loss against [[Newtown Jets|Newtown]].{{cite web|url=https://www.nswrl.com.au/news/2024/09/29/newtown-wins-premiership-number-nine/|title=Newtown wins Premiership number nine|website=www.nswrl.com.au}} == Honours and achievements ==
